igi.help Mar 5, 2008 CONTENTS Ptolemy Ptolemy on the EECS Instructional Systems Ptolemy ------- Ptolemy (http://ptolemy.eecs.berkeley.edu) is an EECS research project, supported by the Ptolemy group. Ptolemy uses mixed models

d by the Ptolemy group. Ptolemy uses mixed models of computation for assembling concurrent embedded systems. It is used for a broad range of applications including signal processing, telecommunications, parallel processing, wireless commun

nications, parallel processing, wireless communications, network design, radio astronomy, real time systems, and hardware/software co-design. Ptolemy is also used as a laboratory for signal processing and communications courses. For infor

rocessing and communications courses. For information and downloads, see http://ptolemy.eecs.berkeley.edu/ Ptolemy on the EECS Instructional Systems ----------------------------------------- The current version of Ptolemy (Ptolemy II 7.0.beta)

e current version of Ptolemy (Ptolemy II 7.0.beta) is installed for Instructional users on 9 PCs in 125 Cory (125-1..125-9) 3 Windows Remote Desktop servers (iserver1, iserver2, iserver3) For information about logging oto the Remote Desk

For information about logging oto the Remote Desktop servers, please see http://inst.eecs.berkeley.edu/connecting.html#labs Here is "Tutorial: Building Ptolemy II Models Graphically": http://www.eecs.berkeley.edu/Pubs/TechRpts/2007/EECS-2007-

ww.eecs.berkeley.edu/Pubs/TechRpts/2007/EECS-2007-129.pdf EECS instructors can email "inst@eecs.berkeley.edu" if they need Ptolemy on other computers for a class. Questions about Ptolemy itself can be sent to "ptolemy@ptolemy.eecs". Older

lf can be sent to "ptolemy@ptolemy.eecs". Older versions of Ptolemy for UNIX are installed in /usr/eesww. Those versions do not work on the Instructional computers any more. EECS Instructional Support 378/384/386 Cory, 333 Soda
